content:
- Ramki Gaddipati Office Hours – Processing Services Check-in
- Structured Summary for Digital Twin

- II. Overall Context of the Conversation/Call

- The call comprised a routine check-in for processing services teams, focusing on updates and problem-solving for several operational topics. The team reviewed the progress of documented SOPs and process definitions for customer service requests, specifically addressing statement generation requests. Control reports' enablement and adoption across clients (notably HDFC and Sparrow) was discussed, along with open items for cross-tenant features. UPI pixel validated reversal handling was tracked, outlining bottlenecks and collaboration needed. The group also covered backend migrations (from Hades to Perseus) for refund operations, outstanding office infrastructure requirements (clean rooms), and the speed and readiness of the UPI disputes workbench. Several email/escalation management issues were delegated, ensuring prompt client responses and tracking. The session revolved around operational alignment, blocker tracking, and ensuring all team members were aware of ownership and next steps, especially in preparation for updates to Ramki.

- IV. Key Takeaways from the Call

- SOP Validation Process Initiated:
- The team began validating the customer service SOP with internal stakeholders prior to formal publishing.
(e.g., "validate the SOP and figure out if there are any further changes")

- Control Reports Deployment Blocked by Prioritization:
- Control reports and feature enablement in HDFC and Sparrow have not advanced due to focus elsewhere; cross-tenant rollout to be prioritized this week.
("P2 for this week where we take it up with Ashish")

- UPI Reverse Process Owner and ETA Pending:
- Intake for validated reversals is acknowledged but there’s no ETA; responsibility lies with Athena, engaging multiple teams.
("waiting for an ETA", "pending at Athena now")

- Backend Migration for Refunds Underway:
- Refunds issues, especially for PayZapp, are being tracked; migration efforts are on from Hades to Perseus, with the hope of accelerated resolution.
("moving away from Hades to Perseus")

- Clarification and Escalation for Email Threads:
- Neha is to clarify and close email escalations by confirming responses were already sent, outstanding item is fetching IP logs.
("help me with that", "attach and share")

- Dispute Workbench Performance Improved but Not 100%:
- Speed has increased though not yet optimal, indicating ongoing monitoring.
("It is good now", "No, not 100%")

- Office Logistics Await Confirmation:
- Chennai office ‘clean room’ is under discussion for Genius Bank, no further action from the team’s side.
("review once with Gopa and discussions with the admin team")

- V. Ramki's Insights

- Note: Ramki did not attend or directly contribute in this segment; the team referred repeatedly to pending updates and actions for Ramki. No explicit new insights, opinions, understandings, agreements, or instructions by Ramki are present in the transcript.

- VI. Ramki's Suggested Action Items

- No direct action items were assigned by Ramki in this conversation, as he was not actively present nor did he provide instructions relayed during the call.
- The only references were team members preparing updates or requesting confirmation for Ramki, but not from his own input.

- VII. Jira Ticket

- No Jira tickets recommended.
- Justification: No specific tasks, bug fixes, or feature items were directly requested by Ramki in this meeting; conversation revolved around follow-ups, preparation for his update, and general operational tracking, not actionable development items.

- VIII. Follow Up Questions/Suggestions

- Confirm whether Ramki has further guidance on sequencing SOP validation and formal rollout, once team proposes updates.
- Request Ramki’s input on priority for control report enablement across tenants given current backlog.
- Update Ramki with the ETA on UPI pixel validated reversals from Athena team as soon as available, as this is pending critical process definition.
- If Ramki is to review any escalation cases or specific client correspondence, provide him a concise summary with unresolved actionables for efficient intervention.

attendees: Visa – some as related participants or references)
entities: SOP validation, customer service automation, control reports, feature enablement, HDFC, Sparrow, UPI reversals, Athena team, refunds, PayZapp, Perseus migration, dispute workbench, provisional credit, escalation management, clean room, Genius Bank, email follow-up, workbench performance, process ownership.

title: 2025-03-17_Processing Services Check-in (5)
attendees: Visa – some as related participants or references)
